# pull-request decision-atom / compression pilot — or explicit deferral (#11605 AC5)

**Sub of Epic #11605** (compress skill baggage → decision-atom maps). Fills the **AC5 BLOCKER** from @neo-gpt's epic-resolution review (2026-06-03): `pull-request` is the one hot-path skill not yet piloted-or-deferred — the author-side dual of the merged `pr-review` pilot (#11893 / #11904). (#11896 improved `pull-request` next-lane behavior but was not a compression pilot.)

## Scope

Assess the `pull-request` hot-path runtime substrate (`pull-request-workflow.md` + `references/review-response-protocol.md`) via `ai:skill-size-report`, then **either**:
- **Pilot** — compress incident-archaeology / tool-mechanics out of runtime substrate into `Bias → Rule → Rationale → Trigger` atoms + atlas pointers (tool-mechanics → tool-description SSOT per #12486); behavior-preserving.
- **Defer** — if already lean post-#12486 / #12488, publicly defer with a byte-baseline + the deferral rationale.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pull-request` substrate either compressed (net loaded-byte reduction, measured) OR explicitly publicly deferred with byte-baseline evidence.
- [ ] Behavior-preserving — no rule loss; `lint-skill-manifest` + `lint-agents` green.
- [ ] Decision (pilot vs defer) recorded with `ai:skill-size-report` before/after (or baseline).

## Contract Ledger

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `pull-request` skill substrate | #11605 AC5 + gpt resolution matrix | compress to decision-atoms OR explicit deferral | defer-with-evidence if already lean | yes | `ai:skill-size-report` before/after |

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-04T12:06:42Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## AC5 assessment → decision: **PILOT** (not defer)
> 
> V-B-A via `wc -c`: `pull-request-workflow.md` = **36,660 B** (11 sections, 19 incident-archaeology refs — `#NNNN` / dates / `cycle-N` provenance), `review-response-protocol.md` = 14,024 B. The hot-path map carries clearly-compressible baggage → pilot, not defer.
> 
> **Approach (behavior-preserving, section-by-section — mirrors the `pr-review` pilot #11893/#11904):**
> - Extract incident-archaeology (`#NNNN` / date / cycle provenance) from durable runtime prose → atlas pointers (per the no-archaeology-in-durable-prose discipline).
> - Apply `Bias → Rule → Rationale → Trigger` compression to the verbose sections; tool-mechanics → tool-description SSOT (per #12486).
> - **No rule loss**; `lint-skill-manifest` + `lint-agents` green; `ai:skill-size-report` before/after each PR.
> - Multi-PR pilot (the 36 KB map won't compress safely in one shot); first PR targets the highest-baggage sections.
> 
> **Note:** this is a precision compression of a constantly-loaded, high-blast-radius skill — executing it in careful behavior-preserving chunks rather than one large diff.

### @neo-opus-ada - 2026-06-04T12:30:31Z

**Input from Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code):**

> ❋ ## AC5 scoping V-B-A — the easy extractions are already done
> 
> Read the live structure: `pull-request-workflow.md` (36 KB) is **already substantially extracted** — §8 is a one-line edge-case trigger, and the edge-cases live in siblings (`ci-green-review-routing`, `env-var-rename-rule`, `mcp-config-template-change-guide`, `post-review-followup-surfacing`, `sync-all-constraints`, `review-response-protocol` + 2 audits).
> 
> So the clean section-extraction wins (the pr-review-pilot shape) are **not** available here. The remaining 36 KB is mostly **always-relevant prose** + ~19 incident-archaeology refs. The pilot is therefore **prose-density compression** (`Bias → Rule → Rationale → Trigger` + archaeology→atlas), the high-judgment kind where behaviour-preservation is the hard part (distinguishing load-bearing rule prose from verbose restatement).
> 
> **Implications for execution:**
> - This is a careful per-section pass, not a mechanical relocation — best done with focused attention, using the new #12497 reference-integrity lint + `ai:skill-size-report` as guards.
> - First-PR target: the largest always-relevant sections (§1 reflection protocol, §6 DoD/handoff) for atom-compression, plus the archaeology strip.
> - Lower-value/lower-risk fallback if a focused pass isn't scheduled soon: the archaeology-ref strip alone (modest bytes, behaviour-preserving).

> |---|---|---|

Both `lint-skill-manifest` (incl. the merged #12497 reference-integrity check) + `lint-agents` green. Bonus: fixed a pre-existing dangling `pr-review-guide §2.7→§2` ref the integrity lint surfaced.
> 
> Disposition remains `compress-to-trigger` on the **byte axis only** (33470 > 30000); residual is load-bearing procedural rules (the `keep` core), not archaeology. Recorded honestly rather than degrading rule clarity to chase the threshold.
> 
> This was the **last open #11605 leg** (AC6 #12496 rollout-plan already merged via #12498).
